title: TestSelectTier: add call assertions, config-derived thresholds, and model propagation
analysis:
TestSelectTier in test_harness.py (lines 877-1033) has 4 tests covering _select_tier() and run_full_cycle tier propagation. Six gaps identified:

1. get_buffer_stats mock is never asserted with correct project_id — easy fix, add assert_called_once_with to each _select_tier test.

2. Threshold boundary values (15) are hardcoded from default config (buffer_size_threshold=10 * opus_threshold_ratio=1.5). Should compute from config object so tests survive default changes.

3. No test exercises non-default config. Need a test with e.g. buffer_size_threshold=20, opus_threshold_ratio=2.0 (threshold=40) to prove the multiplication path.

4. test_run_full_cycle_propagates_tier_to_consolidator captures episode_limit/memory_limit via before_return callback but ignores the model kwarg passed to stage.run(). The _mock_stage_run helper accepts model=None in mock_run but discards it. Need to capture and assert model propagation.

5. The isinstance guard (harness.py:417) ensures only MemoryConsolidator gets episode_limit/memory_limit. No test verifies Stage 2 (TaskKnowledgeSync) and Stage 3 (IntegrityCheck) are NOT mutated. These stages don't define episode_limit/memory_limit attrs, so without the guard Python would dynamically add them — a real bug scenario.

6. The guard assertion (line 1023) checks for specific dict keys rather than asserting the dict is truthy. Should be 'assert captured' with a clear failure message.

All changes are test-only. The harness implementation is correct; we're strengthening test coverage. The _mock_stage_run helper needs a capture_call_args parameter to support model kwarg capture (gap 4).

steps.description: Add assert_called_once_with('test-project') to test_select_tier_sonnet, test_select_tier_opus, and test_select_tier_boundary. Each test already mocks harness.buffer.get_buffer_stats as an AsyncMock — add the assertion after the await _select_tier call. This validates gap 1: the implementation passes project_id correctly to get_buffer_stats.
Run pytest on TestSelectTier to verify the get_buffer_stats assertions pass. No implementation change needed — the harness already calls get_buffer_stats(project_id) correctly at line 226.

Refactor hardcoded threshold values in test_select_tier_sonnet, test_select_tier_opus, and test_select_tier_boundary. After creating the config, compute `opus_threshold = config.reconciliation.buffer_size_threshold * config.reconciliation.opus_threshold_ratio` and derive buffer size values from it: sonnet test uses opus_threshold - 10 (well below), opus test uses opus_threshold + 5 (above), boundary test uses int(opus_threshold) (exactly at). Update comments to reference the computed threshold instead of hardcoded 15.
Run pytest on TestSelectTier to verify config-derived thresholds produce identical results to the old hardcoded values. No implementation change needed.

Add test_select_tier_custom_config: create ReconciliationConfig with buffer_size_threshold=20 and opus_threshold_ratio=2.0 (opus threshold = 40). Test two sub-cases: (a) buffer size 30 returns sonnet (below 40), (b) buffer size 50 returns opus (above 40). Assert returned TierConfig has correct model, episode_limit, and memory_limit. Also assert get_buffer_stats called with correct project_id. This validates gap 3.
Run pytest to verify test_select_tier_custom_config passes. The implementation multiplies buffer_size_threshold * opus_threshold_ratio at line 227, so non-default values should work correctly.

Enhance _mock_stage_run helper: add optional capture_call_args: dict | None = None parameter. In mock_run, if capture_call_args is not None, store capture_call_args['model'] = model. This is a minimal, backward-compatible addition that enables model kwarg capture without changing existing call sites.
Run test_mock_stage_run_before_return_callback (line 322) and any other existing tests using _mock_stage_run to verify the helper change doesn't break anything.

Enhance test_run_full_cycle_propagates_tier_to_consolidator: (a) Replace the 'captured' dict assertion from 'assert episode_limit in captured and memory_limit in captured' to 'assert captured, "stages[0].run was never invoked"' (gap 6). (b) Pass capture_call_args=captured to _mock_stage_run for the MemoryConsolidator stage. (c) Add assertion: assert captured['model'] == 'sonnet', verifying the TierConfig.model is forwarded as the model kwarg to stage.run() (gap 4).
Run pytest on the enhanced propagation test. The harness passes model=tier.model at line 424, so the assertion should pass.

Add test_run_full_cycle_does_not_mutate_non_consolidator_stages: Run run_full_cycle with a known tier, then verify that Stage 2 (TaskKnowledgeSync) and Stage 3 (IntegrityCheck) do NOT have episode_limit or memory_limit attributes. Use before_return callbacks on all stages to capture hasattr checks at invocation time (not just after). Assert not hasattr(stage, 'episode_limit') and not hasattr(stage, 'memory_limit') for stages 2 and 3. This validates gap 5: the isinstance guard at line 417 is working correctly.
Run pytest on the new isinstance guard test. The guard at harness.py:417 only sets episode_limit/memory_limit on MemoryConsolidator instances, so Stage 2 and Stage 3 should not have these attributes.

design_decisions.decision: Enhance _mock_stage_run with capture_call_args parameter instead of replacing stage.run with a raw AsyncMock
design_decisions.rationale: The existing _mock_stage_run helper returns a proper StageReport and supports before_return callbacks. Adding capture_call_args preserves this behavior while enabling kwarg capture. Using AsyncMock directly would lose the StageReport construction and before_return pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Compute threshold from config object in tests rather than from ReconciliationConfig class defaults
design_decisions.rationale: Reading from the instantiated config object (config.reconciliation.buffer_size_threshold * config.reconciliation.opus_threshold_ratio) ensures tests stay correct if the config is constructed with overrides. Reading class defaults would re-introduce the hardcoding problem.

design_decisions.decision: Use hasattr checks in before_return callbacks for isinstance guard test (gap 5)
design_decisions.rationale: Checking hasattr at stage.run() invocation time (via before_return) is more precise than checking after run_full_cycle completes. It proves the attributes were never set, not just that they were cleaned up afterward.

design_decisions.decision: Test custom config with a single test method using two _select_tier calls rather than two separate test methods
design_decisions.rationale: A single test with two calls (one below threshold, one above) validates the multiplication logic end-to-end with one config setup. Two tests would duplicate the config boilerplate for minimal additional isolation benefit.

design_decisions.decision: Keep all changes in test_harness.py only — no implementation changes
design_decisions.rationale: All six gaps are test coverage issues. The harness implementation is correct (get_buffer_stats receives project_id, multiplication works, isinstance guard exists, model kwarg is passed). We are strengthening assertions, not fixing bugs.

reuse.how: Reuse for new tests that need a harness with default config. Already builds config, creates harness with all fixtures.
Enhance with capture_call_args parameter and reuse for all stage mocking. Existing callers are unaffected (new param is optional with default None).
Reuse for creating test events in run_full_cycle tests. Already used by the propagation test.
Follow existing test pattern of constructing FusedMemoryConfig(reconciliation=ReconciliationConfig(...)) with explicit overrides. Use for both default and custom config tests.
Follow the same import pattern, fixture usage, and assertion style when adding new tests to the class.
